summary: Frontend testing of 5 bug fixes - ALL PASS. (1) Lagrange L-Points visualization correctly shows Sun-Earth system with all 5 Lagrange points, (2) Learn page HUD is compact at ~54px height, (3) Molniya orbit perigee is outside Earth's surface, (4) Quiz page has Back to Learn button at top, (5) Challenge page has clear instructions and working telemetry.
critical_code_review_comments: Lagrange visualization in EarthScene.jsx (lines 493-580) properly renders Sun, Earth, Earth's orbit, and all 5 L-points with labels and pulsing animations
Learn page HUD in Learn.jsx (lines 115-137) is compact with orbit name, altitude, and velocity displayed in minimal space (~54px height)
Molniya orbit parameters in orbits.js fixed: semiMajor=5.5, eccentricity=0.6 gives perigee=2.2 units (Earth radius=2.0), so orbit doesn't clip through Earth
Quiz.jsx (lines 317-337) has Back to Learn button always visible at top of page, works in both mission selector and active quiz views
CatchUpChallenge.jsx has comprehensive instructions, hints, and telemetry panel showing GAP, SPEED DIFF, speeds, and altitudes that update in real-time
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 5 bug fixes verified: (1) Lagrange visualization shows Sun, Earth, orbit, and L1-L5 points with labels, (2) HUD is compact at ~54px, (3) Molniya orbit perigee is 2.2 units vs Earth radius of 2.0 so it clears Earth, (4) Back to Learn button present on Quiz page, (5) Challenge page telemetry works - lowering orbit increases speed and closes the gap.
